source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two equal point masses $M$ are connected by a massless rigid rod of length $2A$ (a dumbbell) which is constrained to rotate about an axle fixed to the center of the rod at an angle $\theta$. The center of the rod is at the origin of coordinates, the axle along the $z$-axis, and the dumbbell lies in the $xz$-plane at $t=0$. The angular velocity $\omega$ is a constant in time and is directed along the $z$-axis. Calculate all elements of the inertia tensor. (Be sure to specify the coordinate system you use.)

The answer is a full 3x3 matrix with diagonal and off-diagonal terms, indicating more than a single direct lookup or one-step computation

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two equal point masses $M$ are connected by a massless rigid rod of length $2A$ (a dumbbell) which is constrained to rotate about an axle fixed to the center of the rod at an angle $\theta$. The center of the rod is at the origin of coordinates, the axle along the $z$-axis, and the dumbbell lies in the $xz$-plane at $t=0$. The angular velocity $\omega$ is a constant in time and is directed along the $z$-axis. Calculate all elements of the inertia tensor. (Be sure to specify the coordinate system you use.)
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image entry is a PIL object string representation, not a file path or URL
question text does not explicitly mention a figure, but record metadata marks vision relevance as essential
metadata.reasoning_chain: Use a coordinate frame $xyz$ attached to the dumbbell such that the two point masses are in the $xz$-plane. The elements of the inertia tensor about $O$, given by $I_{ij}=\sum_n m_n\left(r^2\delta_{ij}-x_i x_j\right)$, are $$I_{xx}=2MA^2\cos^2\theta, \quad I_{yy}=2MA^2, \quad I_{zz}=2MA^2\sin^2\theta, \quad I_{xy}=I_{yz}=0, \quad I_{zx}=-2MA^2\cos\theta\sin\theta=-MA^2\sin2\theta.$$ Thus $$\mathbb{I}=\begin{pmatrix}2MA^2\cos^2\theta & 0 & -MA^2\sin2\theta \\ 0 & 2MA^2 & 0 \\ -MA^2\sin2\theta & 0 & 2MA^2\sin^2\theta\end{pmatrix}.$$
